exact.contiguous_through_order10_lambda0: Q_(m,n)(0)>0 for every integer 1<=m<=10 and n>=0
exact.fixed_order_transfer: [epsilon_k H_(k,s)>0 for 1<=k<=m, all s] => [epsilon_k R_(k,n)(j_1,...,j_k)>0 for 1<=k<=m]

proof_boundary: This proves contiguous signed Hankel positivity at lambda zero through order ten and the corresponding consecutive-row arbitrary-column signs through order ten. It proves no order above ten and is not PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: The endpoint analysis supplies strict order-ten positivity on the ray beginning at shift four.
rows.proof_boundary: The four smaller endpoint shifts remain negative.

rows.claim: The shifted cooperative lemma propagates the delayed endpoint ray through the heat interval.
rows.proof_boundary: No sign at shifts below four is used.

rows.claim: Direct Arb determinants supply the four missing rows at lambda zero.
rows.proof_boundary: Fixed lambda zero and shifts zero through three only.

rows.claim: The heat ray and finite prefix partition every nonnegative shift.
rows.proof_boundary: Contiguous signed Hankel order ten at lambda zero.

rows.claim: The completed lower base and order-ten theorem give all contiguous layers through ten.
rows.formula: Q_(m,n)(0)>0 for every integer 1<=m<=10 and n>=0
rows.proof_boundary: Fixed lambda zero; no order above ten is claimed.

rows.claim: The finite initial-minor transfer promotes completed contiguous layers through ten to arbitrary columns.
rows.proof_boundary: Consecutive rows and arbitrary increasing columns, orders at most ten.
